summary: Good‑quality audience recording of a high‑energy 1982 show with standout Other One, Little Red Rooster and Candyman performances
review: The tape captures an electric early‑April ’82 concert where the band’s chemistry is heightened by Jerry and Phil swapping sides. Highlights include a piano‑driven, soaring The Other One, a fiery Little Red Rooster with Brent’s scat, and a blistering Candyman; the first set flows strong while the second set is mixed but features an excellent Drums>Space>Other One jam. Recording quality is praised as clear and punchy for an audience source.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (5/6) give five‑star praise for both performance and sound, noting clear audio and many superb moments; a couple of mixed/negative reviews lower the overall average but the show remains strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers call the recording "very fine" and "punchy", noting clear instrument separation despite being an audience tape; no major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as "bang on" with a strong peak at Little Red Rooster; second set is more uneven but contains the celebrated Drums>Space>Other One sequence and a busy Dark Star, ending with a spry Sugar Magnolia.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is energetic – dancing on stage, feeding off Brent’s keyboards, delivering free‑form solos especially in The Other One and Little Red Rooster.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work shines after the historic left/right swap, adding a tighter low‑end interplay with the new stage positions.
band_member_comments.Bob: Brent Mydland provides piano‑driven texture on The Other One, scat vocals on Little Red Rooster, and leads a slide jam in Samson; his vocal contributions are praised throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mydland’s keyboards are highlighted as jazzy and piano‑like, driving several jams and adding melodic depth.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill & Mickey deliver an excellent Drums>Space>Other One segment; their interplay is called “fierce” and a highlight of the second set.
